fact: The Cocoanut Grove's legal capacity was 460 people, but over 1,000 were inside that night
source: National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) — Cocoanut Grove Nightclub Fire

fact: The owner locked or bolted side exits to prevent patrons from leaving without paying their bar tabs
source: Fire in the Grove: The Cocoanut Grove Tragedy and Its Aftermath, John C. Esposito (2005)

fact: A busboy lit a match near artificial palm tree decorations, igniting the fire
source: Widely documented; detailed account in 'Holocaust!: The Shocking Story of the Boston Cocoanut Grove Fire' by Paul Benzaquin (1959)

fact: The main revolving door jammed as panicked crowds crushed against it from both sides
source: National Fire Protection Association investigation report; also detailed in Casey Grant, 'The Cocoanut Grove Nightclub Fire,' NFPA Journal (2002)
National Fire Protection Association — official death toll widely cited as 492

fact: The fire spread across the ceiling and killed hundreds within approximately 15 minutes
source: Fire in the Grove, John C. Esposito (2005); NFPA accounts describe the main fire engulfing the club in roughly 15 minutes
